subject: Ecology
title: Wetland Productivity
description: A quick win: understand Wetland Productivity.

hook.text: Wetlands produce more biomass per square meter than almost any ecosystem on Earth.

buildup.text: Constant water, abundant nutrients, and sunlight drive explosive plant growth.

discovery.text: This productivity supports enormous food webs—from insects to wading birds to alligators.

twist.text: Salt marshes rival tropical rainforests in net primary productivity per unit area.

climax.text: Wetland productivity also feeds ocean fisheries—two-thirds of commercial fish depend on wetlands.

punchline.text: The most productive land on Earth is half underwater.

quiz.question: What fraction of commercial fish species depend on wetlands?
